body:
| | Peter,

In rural communities like ours, we drive to get to work, we drive to get food, we drive to access health care. As a former auto shop owner, Marie knows how important cars are to our daily lives, especially in rural America.

It’s critical that ordinary people can use every tool at their disposal to fix our own things and make them last. That’s why, last week, Marie introduced bipartisan legislation to keep repair costs down and defend our rights to fix our own vehicles.

**This “right to repair” will make a real, tangible difference in the lives of Americans everywhere. Marie needs to know where you stand on this issue, Peter:**
[Do you agree that making your own auto repairs should be affordable and accessible?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103453250/532222793/-2030009389?refcode=MGP_EM_SV_180DO_250307_1_1&refcode2=414bcffb-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTRiY2ZmYi1iMWZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aPibvj0wJU0yXxIgGhqrSPenznVRe7dUF0eYm9ArG2I=&emci=9803adf4-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=414bcffb-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=31138575) | | ****[YES](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103453251/532222794/-2030009389?refcode=MGP_EM_SV_180DO_250307_1_1&refcode2=414bcffb-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MTRiY2ZmYi1iMWZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aPibvj0wJU0yXxIgGhqrSPenznVRe7dUF0eYm9ArG2I=&emci=9803adf4-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=414bcffb-b1fb-ef11-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=31138575)****

_**500 Responses Needed by Midnight**_
This is pocketbook sustainability that just makes sense, Peter. We want to be able to repair and be good stewards of our stuff, and steer away from reliance on cheap and disposable things.

It’s time to empower ordinary Americans to make their own repairs with access to the necessary data and tools. And as technology progresses and new fields emerge, Marie remains dedicated to making sure we don’t leave rural communities or working families behind.

**Help Marie drive this work in the right direction by responding to our quick survey today:**

clean_body: | | Peter, In rural communities like ours, we drive to get to work, we drive to get food, we drive to access health care. As a former auto shop owner, Marie knows how important cars are to our daily lives, especially in rural America. It’s critical that ordinary people can use every tool at their disposal to fix our own things and make them last. That’s why, last week, Marie introduced bipartisan legislation to keep repair costs down and defend our rights to fix our own vehicles. This “right to repair” will make a real, tangible difference in the lives of Americans everywhere. Marie needs to know where you stand on this issue, Peter: | | ** | 500 Responses Needed by Midnight This is pocketbook sustainability that just makes sense, Peter. We want to be able to repair and be good stewards of our stuff, and steer away from reliance on cheap and disposable things. It’s time to empower ordinary Americans to make their own repairs with access to the necessary data and tools. And as technology progresses and new fields emerge, Marie remains dedicated to making sure we don’t leave rural communities or working families behind. Help Marie drive this work in the right direction by responding to our quick survey today: | | 500 Responses Needed by Midnight Thanks for taking the time to respond, Peter. – Team MGP | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. As the representative for Washington’s third Congressional District, she's working to bring the voices of working people back to DC. If Democrats want to flip the House blue in 2026 and issue a critical check on Donald Trump’s power, we MUST hold this seat blue. I’m not exaggerating when I say that the outcome of our race could have lasting impacts on the future of our democracy. We set a goal to raise $7,500 before tonight at midnight, and right now, we’re about $1,792 short. I’ve forwarded the full message from my team about the DCCC’s endorsement below if you have a second to read it. = | | = Forwarded Message from Vasquez HQ: 🚨 BREAKING NEWS: 🚨 The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee just released their first wave of endorsements for the 2026 cycle, and Gabe was named a Frontliner! name: The New Pennsylvania Project
subject: We need to stand together to defend our right to vote
body:
| | |
---
| | Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter!On Tuesday, March 25th, there are two special elections in Pennsylvania. A special election is just like a primary or general election in terms of how you vote, but only voters in the district can cast a ballot. Some voters in Allegheny and some voters in Lancaster will have the opportunity to participate in a special election on Tuesday, March 25. Voters in the 35th State House District, which is located in Allegheny County, and includes Brentwood, Mount Oliver and parts of Pittsburgh, will fill the vacancy due to the passing of State House Representative Matthew Gergely. Voters in the 36th State Senate District, covering much of northern Lancaster County, like Akron, Columbia, Ephrata, Elizabethtown, Lititz, Manheim and 14 townships, will choose a candidate to fill the recently vacated seat of State Sen. Ryan Aument. Voters participating in the SD36 special election should be aware that the Lancaster County Board of Elections has approved polling place changes. Before you head to the polls on Tuesday, March 25, double check your polling location! [You can see the changes here.](https://u44255144.ct.sendgrid.net/ls/click?upn=u001.ekJ0nzuGxxKMd6ADw0n9YufKpkbUiDvc3wE8SsPiUxDhg8o9uYEAe160x6IUYzmd0N9VEN2UIjNf9iDX69LQZhj56xw7ddBwxj0gJQKfR3uiyEustmI8ABjLMd1GZon4DaKbB7o8t76OxXU5-2FxKirofVoF8hGWGhrf7EWGxgx4sjdfHmbadNteXqlOsH-2B1D8H86dQ4-2BktYJGc9z9lPQPU356Lxd5ilBu7fWeWAA9WTBNKygH8zfqHazB9GnPYbYVsHwbSuQocUNI3-2FIHGg6CU6edmQa3Y9lwgMjN5VFo3UrvEY1Vah-2FJ1977t9IknKoWKoQr3XIJXsu8-2BjecbFHzXKbHNQAlkzls9BLe-2Bezxbwrkq1CMXw4MwBcFMKeJETXpq7YEHP9iUdywoZI-2F2U5-2BTpz9OJ60LJJezRtOElp5GVYynM38FivnBVzb2mqANAZFhzWS09uFnUY08lJ5vmFKovQaaVtiaxuvYRGtHpvrflKpccab8WWU2cByz-2FfYX9vX1vWeoEInalTksUCxvfG7boIdA6tauikDVkN5Vg-2Frf5SSsVVuyF-2BJHJh9H6LBfcvJSHhs3H7g8J9QnnlY4r-2BVzbCehIDFsOHL4M-2FVP4gJQq-2BUdGg51m0wIwKYhn-2Fb1WGcMMl81GrCsvTce4fDNHviYksS07YlHGDs2VIrIDw1mAsM5X4WKC6jyuyYY8Sd87mFuGcs-2F1glwTinKpwHgyfUP1p6JJeoJlJihjWLXa3bAHbaqz1LB9TLLEFMxGLa1ywQY08azi-2F8kFh-2FEJIBKvRAPZLRwPLUBIdJpVRuYclTChVPz2yjsT7NX2Q2PBa4MmOD9uwKhNZsUrxU5OJlkcb-2BcFJuFFOm3EDSSiXhvvmiO-2Fs0FOYIQzaNuJPlQPM9XUufGSoQdE7TspWoi1BZW9Z9cZm5NKkVdIjwjiuCa58H700IKSdhqJmchUfPumMUuzI0linVLvgBz4hTtJb2-2FQ4g-2FiDS2KqxUoWp6VBv9445hiRwrHjOdG-2Fz-2B0wj5ESU2ppj3Ten-2B8jIZo7-2BDdGCO9U3LJ-2BERcsXw-2BHBKJ0EGJIkNHjZBjUQShUsswKoj-2FE4302M0qrUApo-2BgCe1yTQDDtrGdDPUkKYRA-2Bfi6-2BvmIRB0VzQQAVfEA-2F2FJDazoLrO-2Bpe18mkkPkSb6XJwXZM1MidMt8K5cx-2Bvr3cV9oDsFSltqndp1Vevq9UQTxPWRY2oq9VYET7-2Fm-2F7T50NtFLkhG7mw-2BetD-2B8yx9ZUrV7-2Bd6q3Xm9LWH-2FhL7c1HiQzVkrQhQVg68OwA-2BRgwOevjI3e-2FE-2F0TlYgQGdeyjKemRgEJUyw-2BLkjxP-2Bm9qXuBS9E69-2Fu3cC7pR2bPEt5-2F-2FSp0CT4xxVjWYscD032wO1JHSVBNkXqNCFi0LYCNbRMSKAH6yhN5LjyWzIYF6ntzvhEto7X38QIz2ZZgPVbPqLjTdRWizSqS5YKhylyUtAmQf197BCOjCrvu1Np8sOrxJ9M6mp1cX5R6rXcNhXdCVAVV2UlsOLXJJGQYruOXODYl5HGs95-2FQHEs6p2ucpi6QjTJCmuLDSiGtlc26G2IhcPzf-2FCBPvWjgUXoEf9UpZXts-2B9DnuWZgQ529MOo6b-2Bt69o7Deumiy-2FZx-2FO2ErrNp-2FlAUoDVV6FKuGuh38pguU9Wd-2B584fSPyLF0iREdP7x5-2FGYDt9EaHFhnPTmqsFxF-2Bmnr-2BR4noe9HStGxgHaqZAuJsV7-2B7vj-2BigjX5pTiBX-2BZoUVAgL87Dj0eXQxdrVKKAcXiu4XTK9T7u0yW8oqRCUSV8qUUbBogZVORiHpdTfKcmTnEph27hmmTu7 # Why Courts Matter Town Hall 401
---
Thursday, March 27th
6:30-7:30PM
Online via Zoom

How do the courts affect our rights and freedoms? Why should we be involved in judicial elections? Our fourth virtual town hall, co-hosted with **Why Courts Matter - Pennsylvania** , will continue to explore the impact and importance of state and federal courts. It will also offer ways to self-educate and get involved.

During the State of the Union on Tuesday night, Trump shook hands with Chief Justice John Roberts and said, “I won’t forget it,” before patting him on the back after the address to Congress. Trump and his administration assume that the U.S. Supreme Court is on his side. This assumption is supported by statements made by Pam Bondi, Trump’s new Attorney General, on Fox, “The Supreme Court is going to back us up, it’s just getting through all these district court judges." This week, the U.S. Supreme Court reminded President Trump that at least one branch of government _can_ stand up to him. This is the second time the Supreme Court has stopped him in his second term. Although lower courts have blocked other parts of his agenda, not complying with a Supreme Court ruling would be considered a Constitutional crisis. In response to this opposition to the administration’s agenda, Musk and other Trump allies are using impeachment threats in an attempt to intimidate federal judges. These threats are specifically directed at federal judges who have ruled against recent Trump policies or those who may potentially hear future challenges against the administration. While the likelihood of actual impeachment proceedings is low, the calls for impeachment and the introduction of resolutions serve a clear purpose: to create an atmosphere of intimidation and pressure on the judiciary. This strategy represents a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict between the executive and judicial branches of government. By threatening to openly defy court orders and attempting to intimidate judges, the administration is undermining the fundamental principles of checks and balances and the rule of law. This has the potential to create a constitutional crisis with far-reaching consequences for the stability and legitimacy of the American democratic system. **Defending Democracy****

The kind of corruption on display at the State of the Union and inherent in the intimidation tactics aimed at the judiciary harms not only the legitimacy of the Court, but the governmental process as a whole. This is about public trust. Respecting the process of government is important to building and maintaining that public trust.
**Defending Democracy: National Trends ** Similar efforts to exert control over the courts can be seen at the state level as well. Over the course of the last few years, the courts in Utah have ruled against some state legislature’s bills. In response, Republican lawmakers were upfront about their intent to impose some controls over the courts. **Defending Democracy in PA****

**In Pennsylvania, voters have the opportunity to help ensure that our state courts remain independent and fair. There are three retention elections for the state Supreme Court this year. The decisions made by Pennsylvanian voters in November will continue to impact residents for years to come. Judicial elections are an important way for citizens to have a say in the composition of the courts. By electing judges who share their values and perspectives, voters can influence the direction of the judiciary and ensure that it reflects the will of the people. Additionally, participating in judicial elections can help to hold judges accountable for their decisions and ensure that they remain responsive to the needs of the community. By staying informed about judicial candidates and casting their votes, citizens can help to safeguard their rights and freedoms and ensure that the judiciary remains a fair and impartial institution. **Local and municipal elections are also on the ballot this year!** Pennsylvanians have the power to hold their representatives accountable. In December, Republicans on the borough council approved an ordinance eliminating the system of voting that elected members based on geographic precincts — a system that, in recent years, resulted in two Democrats joining the council. Under the new system, all council members would be elected based on a majority of all Wrightsville voters, potentially giving Republicans an advantage in future elections. Mayor Tayne Slenker vowed to veto that ordinance. However, the veto never happened. Governor Shapiro understands that the commonwealth can benefit from the experiences and expertise of federal workers. **Trump’s Threat to Cut VA Funding** Recently, DOGE has turned its sights on the VA (Veterans Affairs). The Trump administration's move to end hundreds of VA contracts — initially paused after public outcry — and ongoing layoffs are affecting the nation's veterans, a critical and politically influential constituency. More than 9 million veterans get physical and mental health care from the VA, which is now being examined by Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. The Trump administration has also paused the work of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency’s (CISA) Elections Security and Resilience Division. Last year, when Election Day bomb threats were received in key swing states, Pennsylvania’s top elections officials were ready, thanks to warnings from an arm of the Department of Homeland Security. Local officials knew the threats were likely not credible, thanks to information shared by CISA that the threats had originated in Russia. “ CISA were very valuable to our state and to other states,” Secretary of State Al Schmidt told Pennsylvania senators. “They have a national and global perspective when it comes to cyber security risks and all the rest that each individual state can’t do on its own.”

This signals a renewed willingness from Democrats to work on the issue. Last November House Democrats retained a one-seat majority in the state House. Any bill must go through a GOP-controlled state Senate, meaning compromise is necessary if Democrats want their own priorities to reach the governor’s desk. House Speaker McClinton herself opposes voter ID expansions. However, voter ID expansion now appears to be an issue Democrats are willing to negotiate — if they get other long-awaited election law changes or Democratic priorities in return.

Here’s the truth: it is already illegal for a non-US citizen to vote. Similar to the SAVE Act introduced in Congress, this bill is being introduced to peddle unfounded conspiracies and to sow doubt in our election systems. Additionally, it works to uplift extremist MAGA talking points to make it more difficult for eligible Americans to make their voices heard, including married women, or those without a passport.

clean_body: | | | | | Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New Pennsylvania Project newsletter!On Tuesday, March 25th, there are two special elections in Pennsylvania. A special election is just like a primary or general election in terms of how you vote, but only voters in the district can cast a ballot. Some voters in Allegheny and some voters in Lancaster will have the opportunity to participate in a special election on Tuesday, March 25. Voters in the 35th State House District, which is located in Allegheny County, and includes Brentwood, Mount Oliver and parts of Pittsburgh, will fill the vacancy due to the passing of State House Representative Matthew Gergely. Voters in the 36th State Senate District, covering much of northern Lancaster County, like Akron, Columbia, Ephrata, Elizabethtown, Lititz, Manheim and 14 townships, will choose a candidate to fill the recently vacated seat of State Sen. Ryan Aument. Voters participating in the SD36 special election should be aware that the Lancaster County Board of Elections has approved polling place changes. Before you head to the polls on Tuesday, March 25, double check your polling location! If you are registered in either district, make your plan to vote! Polls are open from 7am to 8pm. If you are in line by 8pm, stay in line and exercise your freedom to vote.#WhenWeVoteWeWin | | | | | | | | Why Courts Matter Town Hall 401 Thursday, March 27th 6:30-7:30PM Online via Zoom How do the courts affect our rights and freedoms? Why should we be involved in judicial elections? Our fourth virtual town hall, co-hosted with Why Courts Matter - Pennsylvania , will continue to explore the impact and importance of state and federal courts. It will also offer ways to self-educate and get involved. | | | | | From : "Happy , y’all! And while I’m always ecstatic to see the outpour of support this time of year for so many amazing women, let’s not forget what it means to celebrate and support the success, rights, and work of ALL women (not just the ones you like, are comfortable with, or find attractive my dudes). And I’m talking ALL women: Black, Indigenous, queer, trans, disabled, fat, poor, refugee and houseless! PS: too often this time of year we see baseless promises from brands and influencers...jumping on every opportunity to market and sell the story when it’s convenient (one that’s unsurprisingly white, skinny, and privileged), without acknowledging the very real systemic barrier. And as we know, folks...if it isn’t intersectional, it isn’t feminism! So keep a watchful eye out this month and keep holding brands and those around you accountable to “supporting” women more than a couple days a year!" | | | During the State of the Union on Tuesday night, Trump shook hands with Chief Justice John Roberts and said, “I won’t forget it,” before patting him on the back after the address to Congress. Trump and his administration assume that the U.S. Supreme Court is on his side. This assumption is supported by statements made by Pam Bondi, Trump’s new Attorney General, on Fox, “The Supreme Court is going to back us up, it’s just getting through all these district court judges." This week, the U.S. Supreme Court reminded President Trump that at least one branch of government can stand up to him. A divided U.S. in a 5-4 vote. This is the second time the Supreme Court has stopped him in his second term. Although lower courts have blocked other parts of his agenda, not complying with a Supreme Court ruling would be considered a Constitutional crisis. In response to this opposition to the administration’s agenda, Musk and other Trump allies are using impeachment threats in an attempt to intimidate federal judges. These threats are specifically directed at federal judges who have ruled against recent Trump policies or those who may potentially hear future challenges against the administration. While the likelihood of actual impeachment proceedings is low, the calls for impeachment and the introduction of resolutions serve a clear purpose: to create an atmosphere of intimidation and pressure on the judiciary. This strategy represents a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict between the executive and judicial branches of government. By threatening to openly defy court orders and attempting to intimidate judges, the administration is undermining the fundamental principles of checks and balances and the rule of law. This has the potential to create a constitutional crisis with far-reaching consequences for the stability and legitimacy of the American democratic system. Defending Democracy — is occurring while the public’s trust in the judiciary is at a . The kind of corruption on display at the State of the Union and inherent in the intimidation tactics aimed at the judiciary harms not only the legitimacy of the Court, but the governmental process as a whole. This is about public trust. Respecting the process of government is important to building and maintaining that public trust. Defending Democracy: National Trends Similar efforts to exert control over the courts can be seen at the state level as well. Over the course of the last few years, the courts in Utah have ruled against some state legislature’s bills. In response, Republican lawmakers were upfront about their intent to impose some controls over the courts. were introduced in the Utah legislature that attempted to make it easier to vote judges off the bench. This attempt by the Utah legislature invites partisan politics into the judicial system. The Chief Justice of the Utah Supreme Court hand-delivered a letter to members of the Utah Judicial Council — the group of judges that governs the state courts — calling HB512 a “dangerous” precedent that would undermine public trust in the courts. Defending Democracy in PA In Pennsylvania, voters have the opportunity to help ensure that our state courts remain independent and fair. There are three retention elections for the state Supreme Court this year. The decisions made by Pennsylvanian voters in November will continue to impact residents for years to come. Judicial elections are an important way for citizens to have a say in the composition of the courts. By electing judges who share their values and perspectives, voters can influence the direction of the judiciary and ensure that it reflects the will of the people. Additionally, participating in judicial elections can help to hold judges accountable for their decisions and ensure that they remain responsive to the needs of the community. By staying informed about judicial candidates and casting their votes, citizens can help to safeguard their rights and freedoms and ensure that the judiciary remains a fair and impartial institution. Local and municipal elections are also on the ballot this year! Pennsylvanians have the power to hold their representatives accountable. In December, Republicans on the borough council approved an ordinance eliminating the system of voting that elected members based on geographic precincts — a system that, in recent years, resulted in two Democrats joining the council. Under the new system, all council members would be elected based on a majority of all Wrightsville voters, potentially giving Republicans an advantage in future elections. Mayor Tayne Slenker vowed to veto that ordinance. However, the veto never happened. , a necessary step in the governing process. In that vacuum, the York County Board of Elections adopted the council’s at-large election model on Wednesday, opening four council seats for the May 20 primary. Those seats would be elected by all registered Wrightsville voters, as opposed to voters from individual precincts. Learn more about how to take action this year and help keep courts independent and accountable to the people at our next | | | | | | Mondays @ McCormick's On Monday, March 3rd, founding CEO of the New Pennsylvania Project, Kadida Kenner, was invited by to participate in their accountability protest. Kadida spoke about this week's theme:Justice and Judges. The Mondays@McCormick movement is a weekly accountability protest held every Monday at noon through April – rain or shine – outside McCormick's office at 2000 Market Street in Philadelphia. Voters across the country have organized protests outside the offices of elected officials and have attended town hall meetings to express their frustration with the current administration’s actions. | | | | Why Courts Matter 301 | | | On Thursday, February 27th, more than a hundred participants joined our third virtual town hall alongside Why Courts Matter - Pennsylvania. The town hall focused on the importance of our federal courts and the role elected officials play in nominating and confirming judges. | | | | Staff members of the affiliated organization, New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF), continue to provide voters with civic education across the Commonwealth. Did you know that in Pennsylvania the overwhelming majority of the formerly incarcerated have the right to vote, the moment they are released? These two gentlemen are returning citizens. One of our star canvassers talked to them about their rights and let them know about organizations, like us, that can offer them employment assistance. They both happily registered to vote! Dauphin County | | | | | Two gentlemen who were recently released from prison register to vote in Harrisburg. | | Philadelphia | | Our team in Philadelphia connected with young voters at Lincoln High School's Union Experience Fair. | | | Allegheny County | | | | | Free cookies and civic education in North Pittsburgh | Your voice matters! Thanks to the efforts of voters like you, instructing their members to avoid public meetings altogether. Instead of facing the people they represent, Republican lawmakers are backing away from open forums. Leaders are advising them to move to pre-screened, invite-only events or shift entirely to virtual town halls where they can control who speaks. Make sure that you keep yourself informed and hold your representatives accountable! Civic engagement is more than just voting twice a year!It’s paying attention and showing up! Here’s somenews you can use to keep yourself informed! Federal Workers Fired by Trump: sudden deep cuts in the federal workforce, made at the behest of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), have left workers uncertain and in fear. The current administration’s attack on the federal workforce has caused a great deal of chaos, including here in Pennsylvania. Impact in PA In response to the administration’s attack on the federal labor force, . Governor Shapiro understands that the commonwealth can benefit from the experiences and expertise of federal workers. Trump’s Threat to Cut VA Funding Recently, DOGE has turned its sights on the VA (Veterans Affairs). The Trump administration's move to end hundreds of VA contracts — initially paused after public outcry — and ongoing layoffs are affecting the nation's veterans, a critical and politically influential constituency. More than 9 million veterans get physical and mental health care from the VA, which is now being examined by Musk's Department of Government Efficiency. Some Veterans’ Administration healthcare workers, for instance, were told they were immune from reductions - but crisis line call-takers as well as medical researchers for cancer, prosthetics, and other fields. Impact in PA Pennsylvania is home to a disproportionate share of the nation’s veterans, more than 800,000, many of whom depend on the VA for medical care, including mental health treatment, as well as housing. Trump to sell Federal Buildings The Trump Administration was seeking to sell 320 federal buildings across the US — including half of the nearly two dozen properties owned in Pennsylvania. The Trump administration the Main Justice building on Pennsylvania Avenue in Washington, D.C., on a list of properties it intends to put on the market. (The reason? The property is “not core to government operations.”) Impact in PA In counties throughout the state, from Erie to Philadelphia, at least 10 federal facilities were placed on the General Services Administration’s (GSA) out of the , signaling plans to sell the buildings. Notable cuts include several Social Security Administration buildings, a Veterans Administration Center in Philadelphia and Pittsburgh’s William S. Moorhead skyscraper. Election News: Federal Cuts to Election Security Agencies & Threat to Vote by Mail Trump has repeatedly said he would like to end vote by mail, falsely claiming it allows for widespread fraud. would absorb the U.S. Postal Services (USPS), an independent agency, into the administration, thus dissolving the service’s leadership. This executive order has the potential to make voting by mail— an option by tens of millions of voters last year — more difficult. The Trump administration has also paused the work of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency’s (CISA) Elections Security and Resilience Division. Last year, when Election Day bomb threats were received in key swing states, Pennsylvania’s top elections officials were ready, thanks to warnings from an arm of the Department of Homeland Security. Local officials knew the threats were likely not credible, thanks to information shared by CISA that the threats had originated in Russia. “ CISA were very valuable to our state and to other states,” Secretary of State Al Schmidt told Pennsylvania senators. “They have a national and global perspective when it comes to cyber security risks and all the rest that each individual state can’t do on its own.” Election News: Pennsylvania - Statewide "Pennsylvania is spending more than $10 million to replace its electronic voter registration database with an election management system that will also provide election night results and handle campaign finance filings and lobbyist registration. Secretary of State Al Schmidt announced Wednesday the Civix system should be fully in use by the time of the next presidential election in 2028.” work with the Department of State to replace the Statewide Uniform Registry of Electors, commonly known as the SURE system, that has been in use in the Commonwealth since 2003. The new system will be implemented in phases and will ultimately provide a streamlined, one-stop-shop elections administration experience for the Department, county elections boards, candidates, political committees, and lobbyists. According to the House Speaker Joanna McClinton said she was “open to including stricter voter identification requirements as part of a broader effort to modernize the state’s election law,” which includes allowing more time to process mail ballots and clean up the election code. This signals a renewed willingness from Democrats to work on the issue. Last November House Democrats retained a one-seat majority in the state House. Any bill must go through a GOP-controlled state Senate, meaning compromise is necessary if Democrats want their own priorities to reach the governor’s desk. House Speaker McClinton herself opposes voter ID expansions. However, voter ID expansion now appears to be an issue Democrats are willing to negotiate — if they get other long-awaited election law changes or Democratic priorities in return. | | | | | The right to vote is at stake for millions in PA A bill aimed at restricting voter access was already introduced in the PA House. , introduced by Republican Representative David Maloney (Berks), would establish new proof of citizenship requirements for Pennsylvanians registering to vote. Here’s the truth: it is already illegal for a non-US citizen to vote. Similar to the SAVE Act introduced in Congress, this bill is being introduced to peddle unfounded conspiracies and to sow doubt in our election systems. Additionally, it works to uplift extremist MAGA talking points to make it more difficult for eligible Americans to make their voices heard, including married women, or those without a passport. As a voting rights organization, we are vehemently against making it harder to register to vote and adding unnecessary barriers to this process that will have negative impacts on the communities we center. You can find the email addresses and Facebook pages for all members of the state legislature . Please contact at least one and let them know that their constituents want them to stop this massive attack on our right to make our voices heard. | | | Last day to register to vote for the Primary Election: Monday, May 5, 2025 Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20, 2025 Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20, 2025 Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28, 2025 General Election: Tuesday, November 4, 2025 | Important Links: Voter Hotlines: Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTEDOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTAAsian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US | | Thank you for staying informed and engaged!We wish those who celebrate a blessed Ramadan. name: Tammy Baldwin

body:
This race is going to be close, and our rights and freedoms depend on it.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39498%2E3422442%2EWr6Lxg)
Wisconsin U.S. Senator Tammy Baldwin here, Derek.
I’m no stranger to how close statewide races in Wisconsin can be. Last fall, my race was the most expensive Senate race our state has ever seen — and it all came down to just a few votes. **This year, the stakes are just as high.**
**Right now, right-wing special interests are pouring millions into Wisconsin to try to buy a seat on our Supreme Court. And now, Elon Musk has weighed in—making it clear that the far right is all-in on flipping control of the court.**
_We can't let them win._ We have a chance to elect Judge Susan Crawford, a dedicated public servant who will protect our rights and uphold the law. But with the GOP’s megadonors opening their checkbooks, we have to fight back with everything we’ve got.
**[Can you split a donation between Susan Crawford’s campaign and Truth to Power? Your support is critical if we want Susan to win this race.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250307-2?refcode=em20250307-39498&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39498_3422442_Wr6Lxg&akid=39498%2E3422442%2EWr6Lxg)**
[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250307-2?refcode=em20250307-39498&refcode2=39498_3422442_Wr6Lxg&akid=39498%2E3422442%2EWr6Lxg)
Reproductive rights, funding for public education, voting rights, and so much more is on the line in this race. It’s already clear that this will not only be one of the closest races in the country, but it will also be the biggest race of 2025—and we know that every dollar makes a difference.
If we all step up now, we can counter right-wing spending and keep Wisconsin’s Supreme Court fair and independent.
**[I hope you’ll join me in supporting Susan Crawford for Wisconsin Supreme Court by splitting a donation with Truth to Power. If you can, chip in $5, $10, or whatever you’re able to help keep our progressive majority.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ttp-em-20250307-2?refcode=em20250307-39498&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39498_3422442_Wr6Lxg&akid=39498%2E3422442%2EWr6Lxg)**
Thank you,
Tammy

Former U.S. Congresswoman Katie Porter founded Truth to Power to support the campaigns of her fellow Democrats. Truth to Power supports candidates who will stand up to Donald Trump, fight to lower costs, and protect the environment. **[Donate ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/truth-to-power-1?refcode=em20250307-39498&amounts=&t=2&refcode2=39498_3422442_Wr6Lxg&akid=39498%2E3422442%2EWr6Lxg)**

clean_body: This race is going to be close, and our rights and freedoms depend on it. Wisconsin U.S. Senator Tammy Baldwin here, Derek. I’m no stranger to how close statewide races in Wisconsin can be. Last fall, my race was the most expensive Senate race our state has ever seen — and it all came down to just a few votes. This year, the stakes are just as high. Right now, right-wing special interests are pouring millions into Wisconsin to try to buy a seat on our Supreme Court. And now, Elon Musk has weighed in—making it clear that the far right is all-in on flipping control of the court. We can't let them win. We have a chance to elect Judge Susan Crawford, a dedicated public servant who will protect our rights and uphold the law. But with the GOP’s megadonors opening their checkbooks, we have to fight back with everything we’ve got. Reproductive rights, funding for public education, voting rights, and so much more is on the line in this race. It’s already clear that this will not only be one of the closest races in the country, but it will also be the biggest race of 2025—and we know that every dollar makes a difference. If we all step up now, we can counter right-wing spending and keep Wisconsin’s Supreme Court fair and independent. Thank you, Tammy Former U.S. Congresswoman Katie Porter founded Truth to Power to support the campaigns of her fellow Democrats. Truth to Power supports candidates who will stand up to Donald Trump, fight to lower costs, and protect the environment. Paid for by Truth to Power PAC

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Two new wins in court!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642
body:
Judges blocked the Trump administration’s federal funding freeze and cuts to medical research.
# [](https://act.robbonta.com/go/3?refcode=em20250307&t=1&akid=1679%2E1088799%2EvHpDVi)
Big news: **Yesterday, we secured a court order to continue blocking President Trump’s unlawful federal funding freeze!**
This is another step toward victory against Trump’s illegal power grab. For now, a U.S. District Court ruled that the Trump administration cannot implement this freeze while litigation continues.
If allowed to go into effect, the funding freeze would threaten _hundreds of billions of dollars_ California receives annually for vital public safety, healthcare, childcare, and infrastructure funding, as well as other essential services that our communities rely on.
**We also secured a temporary restraining order to prevent Trump from implementing massive funding cuts to the National Institutes of Health (NIH)** , which conducts life-saving medical research.
**[These are promising wins, but the fight isn’t over. Will you pitch in $5 or whatever you can afford today to keep fueling our grassroots campaign?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-20250307?refcode=em20250307-1679&amount=5&refcode2=1679_1088799_vHpDVi&akid=1679%2E1088799%2EvHpDVi)**

Thanks for anything you can give to support me as I fight for you.
In solidarity,
Rob

subject: Roger Stone’s latest attack on Zoe (please read, don’t delete)

body:
Click here to donate to reelect Representative Zoe Lofgren >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q12025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-03-07_ZOL_RST_ES-18520&amount=25]

Dear Friend –

Ever since Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren’s courageous work to hold Donald Trump accountable on the January 6th Committee, MAGA Republicans have unleashed unprecedented attacks on Zoe.
Stand with Zoe in her fight to protect our democracy against Donald Trump >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q12025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-03-07_ZOL_RST_ES-18520&amount=25]

Trump loyalist Roger Stone has been leading the attacks on Zoe to tarnish her name, character, and reputation – he even funneled large amounts of money into the race last year to try and unseat her.
And he hasn’t stopped since – all because Zoe refuses to bow down to Donald Trump and his attacks on our democracy!

So friend, we need Zoe in Congress leading the fight against Donald Trump and Republican extremists.

Please chip in $25 today to keep Zoe in Congress. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/e-zoe-lofgren-q12025?refcode=EM_ALL_ZOL_FR_1_2025-03-07_ZOL_RST_ES-18520&amount=25]

clean_body: Click here to donate to reelect Representative Zoe Lofgren >> [] Dear Friend – Ever since Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren’s courageous work to hold Donald Trump accountable on the January 6th Committee, MAGA Republicans have unleashed unprecedented attacks on Zoe. Stand with Zoe in her fight to protect our democracy against Donald Trump >> [] Trump loyalist Roger Stone has been leading the attacks on Zoe to tarnish her name, character, and reputation – he even funneled large amounts of money into the race last year to try and unseat her. And he hasn’t stopped since – all because Zoe refuses to bow down to Donald Trump and his attacks on our democracy! So friend, we need Zoe in Congress leading the fight against Donald Trump and Republican extremists. Please chip in $25 today to keep Zoe in Congress. [] If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your contribution will go through immediately: EXPRESS DONATE: $5 >> [] Thank you for standing with Zoe in the fight to protect our democracy. Friends of Zoe Lofgren Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren is a strong fighter for California’s values in Washington. As the Chair of the California Democratic Congressional Delegation, she has been on the front lines of expanding and protecting the Democratic Majority. Zoe has dedicated her career to fighting for our diverse communities and needs your help. Team, Sixty years ago, a group of brave Americans – led by the late Congressman John Lewis – gathered in Selma, Alabama, to march toward the state capital in Montgomery in defense of the voting rights that were being denied to African Americans. On the way out of the city, the group was attacked and beaten by local police and an angry mob – an event that became known as “Bloody Sunday”. **Despite the difficulties they faced, these brave protesters continued their fight to ensure that ALL Americans received the voting rights they were constitutionally guaranteed.** The challenges these protesters faced spurred the development of the historic Voting Rights Act, which prohibited racial discrimination in voting and helped implement changes to expand ballot access for African Americans and other minority groups. **It has been a long fight to secure the freedom to vote in this country, team.** My great-great-great-grandfather gained his voting rights in 1867. He and my ancestors fought hard for freedom and equality, and their sacrifices and struggles inspired me as I ran to become the first woman and person of color to represent Delaware in the Senate. I even wore a scarf imprinted with the document that gave my great-great-grandfather his voting rights when I was sworn in. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103453746/532244421/-1694324749?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.03.07_Bloody-Sunday_B1_F1_S1_C1_210DONx90DO_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNDk5MThmMy1iN2ZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=drrE_GUG2WfGhamI7uvPaDzM2HY29efoGtIBAKKTXY8=) **Today, the fight to guarantee voting rights continues.** Dangerous misinformation about our elections from the President and his allies are leading to attacks on ballot access. And right now, the MAGA movement in Congress is trying to pass a bill whose voter ID requirements are so strict, they could disenfranchise _**millions**_ of Americans. [**Team, the right to vote is a fundamental part of our democracy – and we need to make sure that right, and so many others, are protected. Will you please make a grassroots donation of any amount you can spare today to help me have the resources I’ll need to defend voting rights?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103453747/532244422/-1694324749?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.03.07_Bloody-Sunday_B1_F1_S1_C1_210DONx90DO_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNDk5MThmMy1iN2ZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=drrE_GUG2WfGhamI7uvPaDzM2HY29efoGtIBAKKTXY8=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | ****[CHIP IN $5 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103453748/532244423/-1694324749?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode2=EL5&refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.03.07_Bloody-Sunday_B1_F1_S1_C1_210DONx90DO_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNDk5MThmMy1iN2ZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=drrE_GUG2WfGhamI7uvPaDzM2HY29efoGtIBAKKTXY8=)****

Hi Peter,

Trump spent his joint address claiming that America is _“on the verge of a great comeback.”_

**Here’s the truth: Trump is taking us backward.** Our evidence?
* Inflation is up.
* Consumer confidence is down.
* He is attacking critical programs like Medicaid and SNAP that will hurt our most vulnerable citizens.
* His attitude toward Ukraine will only put Americans, and democracy as a whole, in danger.
* He’s surrounding himself with fellow billionaires and cronies who won’t tell him no.

The working and middle class will be the ones to suffer the consequences, unless we stand up and push back _**now**_**. That means organizing —[can you donate here to help make that possible?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103454042/532249110/-1784870395?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_PADM_20250307_FR_TrumpSpeech_0_3MO_PADems_NA_NA_1.3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ3MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxN2E3OWEwNy1iOWZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5AXWJ_YarCVD5j8Zm0PSPDsdolAyow10Kuls-wOAv20=)** | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

The PA Democratic Party plans to put a check on Trump’s power by protecting our rights at the state level.

From our special elections this month, to state Court elections in November, to next year’s midterms, our state has many opportunities in the next 18 months to push back against Trump’s authoritarian takeover.

But we can’t do it alone. We have to show that the people of PA are with us. **And we have to make sure people vote.

[Donate anything you can right now to help us get our grassroots support off the ground. The more work we put in now, the better chance we have of taking on Trump and saving our democracy.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/103454049/532249144/-1784870395?amount=10&source=EM_NBI_PADM_20250307_FR_TrumpSpeech_0_3MO_PADems_NA_NA_1.3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ3MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxN2E3OWEwNy1iOWZiLWVmMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5AXWJ_YarCVD5j8Zm0PSPDsdolAyow10Kuls-wOAv20=)**

clean_body: | | Hi Peter, Trump spent his joint address claiming that America is “on the verge of a great comeback.” Here’s the truth: Trump is taking us backward. Our evidence? Inflation is up. Consumer confidence is down. He is attacking critical programs like Medicaid and SNAP that will hurt our most vulnerable citizens. His attitude toward Ukraine will only put Americans, and democracy as a whole, in danger. He’s surrounding himself with fellow billionaires and cronies who won’t tell him no. The working and middle class will be the ones to suffer the consequences, unless we stand up and push back now. That means organizing —= | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = The PA Democratic Party plans to put a check on Trump’s power by protecting our rights at the state level. From our special elections this month, to state Court elections in November, to next year’s midterms, our state has many opportunities in the next 18 months to push back against Trump’s authoritarian takeover. But we can’t do it alone. We have to show that the people of PA are with us. And we have to make sure people vote. = Thank you, PA Dems = | Follow us on , , and ! name: Mazie Hirono
subject: Republicans don’t give a rip about you
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Let’s not mince words, Peter: **If you’re not one of their billionaire buddies, Republicans don’t give a rip about you.** Donald Trump’s highest priority is passing a massive tax break for billionaires — and he’s given Elon Musk, the richest man in the world, the green light to steal your children’s future to pay for it. Right now, Elon has set his sights on gutting the Department of Education to pay for massive tax cuts. If the Department of Education is shut down, it will not be Elon or Trump who suffer the consequences: **it will be our children. ** And meanwhile, Trump’s pick to lead the Department of Education has expressed that she “wholeheartedly supports” his plans to dissolve the agency. **Donald Trump, Elon Musk, and their allies are toying with millions of kids’ futures to line their own pockets. ** We cannot let them rob our children, Peter. That’s why I’m fighting every day in the Senate to protect the American people from their dangerous ideas. **[But I can’t do it alone. Donald Trump, Elon Musk, and their allies are toying with millions of kids’ futures to line their own pockets. We cannot let them rob our children, Peter. name: Senator Vincent Hughes
subject: What's Going on In Washington DC: HEALTHCARE
body:
If you are having trouble viewing this message or would like to share it on a social network, you can [view the message online](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1).
| |
---
| Share On | [](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=share&piv=2100169655.172159.24&snid=2)
---|---
| | [](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13879)
---
| | | | |
#
# **What 's Going on In Washington DC:**
# **HEALTHCARE**
* * *
_Republicans in Washington DC have taken the first step toward cutting healthcare for millions. They agreed on a framework that requires a $2 TRILLION cut from the budget to fund tax cuts for billionaires. Medicaid, the Affordable Care Act, and lower prescription drug costs are all on the chopping block. _ __ Federal Republicans want to make [BIG cuts to the budget to fund tax cuts for billionaires and millionaires](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13880). They're considering a cut to healthcare, education, the childcare tax credit, tax credits for homeowners, and other programs that support low and middle income earners. They're working hard to figure out how to put more money in the pockets of billionaires and millionaires, _instead_ of the pockets of people living on a fixed income. The first item on their chopping block will likely be healthcare. [**It 's time to get activated!**](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13881)
* * *
**WATCH:**[**SENATOR HUGHES SHARES A CALL TO ACTION**](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13882) [](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13882)
* * *
**Senator Hughes ' Telephone Townhall on Federal Attacks on Healthcare** Senator Hughes recently hosted a telephone townhall with experts from the [Pennsylvania Health Access Network](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13883) and the [Center for American Progress](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13884). He heard from constituents who are concerned about losing their healthcare and constituents who wanted to educate themselves about what cuts and plans to downsize government services could mean for them. [**LISTEN TO THE TELEPHONE TOWNHALL ONLINE**](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13885) The 2 biggest takeaways from the discussion:
1. **Any cuts to healthcare will have an impact on the entire healthcare system, even if lawmakers claim the cuts are targeted. **Hospitals and doctors will be overextended, insurance could get more expensive.
2. **This fight is not over**. We need to call our federal lawmakers and tell them to keep their hands off our healthcare. If these lawmakers can see that people are paying attention and not happy, it will have an impact on their votes and negotiations.

In Pennsylvania, Senator Hughes has introduced multiple bills to make quality healthcare more accessible and less costly. Call your [federal lawmakers](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13881) and ask them to commit to doing the same thing. **[Call your Congressperson!](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13881)** [](http://enews.senatorhughes.com/mail/util.cfm?mailaction=clickthru&gpiv=2100169655.172159.24&gen=1&mailing_linkid=13881)
---

clean_body: If you are having trouble viewing this message or would like to share it on a social network, you can . | | | Share On | ---|--- | | | | | | | What 's Going on In Washington DC: HEALTHCARE Republicans in Washington DC have taken the first step toward cutting healthcare for millions. They agreed on a framework that requires a $2 TRILLION cut from the budget to fund tax cuts for billionaires. Medicaid, the Affordable Care Act, and lower prescription drug costs are all on the chopping block. Federal Republicans want to make . They're considering a cut to healthcare, education, the childcare tax credit, tax credits for homeowners, and other programs that support low and middle income earners. They're working hard to figure out how to put more money in the pockets of billionaires and millionaires, instead of the pockets of people living on a fixed income. The first item on their chopping block will likely be healthcare. WATCH: Senator Hughes ' Telephone Townhall on Federal Attacks on Healthcare Senator Hughes recently hosted a telephone townhall with experts from the and the . He heard from constituents who are concerned about losing their healthcare and constituents who wanted to educate themselves about what cuts and plans to downsize government services could mean for them. The 2 biggest takeaways from the discussion: 1. Any cuts to healthcare will have an impact on the entire healthcare system, even if lawmakers claim the cuts are targeted. Hospitals and doctors will be overextended, insurance could get more expensive. 2. This fight is not over. We need to call our federal lawmakers and tell them to keep their hands off our healthcare. If these lawmakers can see that people are paying attention and not happy, it will have an impact on their votes and negotiations. In Pennsylvania, Senator Hughes has introduced multiple bills to make quality healthcare more accessible and less costly. Call your and ask them to commit to doing the same thing. ** | | Offices of Senator Vincent Hughes | | | | | | District Office 2401 North 54th Street Philadelphia, PA 19131 Phone: 215.879.7777 Fax: 215.879.7778 Harrisburg Office Senate Box 203007 Harrisburg, PA 17120-3007 Phone: 717.787.7112 Fax: 717.772.0579 | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | \- \-

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas A &M Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor**," Bryan Eagle's **Stephen Whitaker** -- "The Texas A&M University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas Comptroller**Glenn Hegar** as the 15th chancellor in system history.

Hegar, a 1993 A&M graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. **Trent Ashby** , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M Foundation President **Tyson Voelkel** ; University of Alabama President **Stuart Bell** ; and U.S. Rep. **Michael McCaul** , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M grads.

"The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M University System," Board of Regents Chairman **Bill Mahomes** said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. "Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System's impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/c5dfbfd6-c616-4ee2-a420-789e727b8e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1.

"The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M University System is a tremendous honor," Hegar said in a statement. "I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/c5dfbfd6-c616-4ee2-a420-789e727b8e3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current Chancellor**John Sharp** announced he would retire on June 30, 2025.

Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon.

"Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best."

Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history.

"I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M University System to heights that no one thought possible," Hegar said in a statement on X.

A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston.

"The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates," Mahomes said in a statement. "We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process."

Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state's chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller's office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller's office also handles the state's cash and the state's Treasury Safekeeping Trust.

Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan.

Hegar graduated from A&M with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary's University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. ..

Gov. **Greg Abbott** will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." [Bryan Eagle](https://substack.com/redirect/d80f2407-371b-48bd-bd78-4f0e4a458d4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Texas A &M Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor," Bryan Eagle's Stephen Whitaker -- "The Texas A&M University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas ComptrollerGlenn Hegar as the 15th chancellor in system history. Hegar, a 1993 A&M graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. Trent Ashby , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M Foundation President Tyson Voelkel ; University of Alabama President Stuart Bell ; and U.S. Rep. Michael McCaul , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M grads. "The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M University System," Board of Regents Chairman Bill Mahomes said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. "Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System's impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish." Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1. "The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M University System is a tremendous honor," Hegar said in a statement. "I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence." The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current ChancellorJohn Sharp announced he would retire on June 30, 2025. Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon. "Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best." Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history. "I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M University System to heights that no one thought possible," Hegar said in a statement on X. A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston. "The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates," Mahomes said in a statement. "We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process." Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state's chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller's office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller's office also handles the state's cash and the state's Treasury Safekeeping Trust. Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan. Hegar graduated from A&M with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary's University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. .. Gov. Greg Abbott will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." * **TOP NEWS**

"**Texas A &M Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor**," Bryan Eagle's **Stephen Whitaker** -- "The Texas A&M University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas Comptroller**Glenn Hegar** as the 15th chancellor in system history.

Hegar, a 1993 A&M graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. **Trent Ashby** , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M Foundation President **Tyson Voelkel** ; University of Alabama President **Stuart Bell** ; and U.S. Rep. **Michael McCaul** , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M grads.

"The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M University System," Board of Regents Chairman **Bill Mahomes** said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. "Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System's impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/11a647f8-7628-43c9-b05f-2a134a0d4c02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1.

"The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M University System is a tremendous honor," Hegar said in a statement. "I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/11a647f8-7628-43c9-b05f-2a134a0d4c02?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current Chancellor**John Sharp** announced he would retire on June 30, 2025.

Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon.

"Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best."

Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history.

"I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M University System to heights that no one thought possible," Hegar said in a statement on X.

A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston.

"The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates," Mahomes said in a statement. "We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process."

Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state's chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller's office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller's office also handles the state's cash and the state's Treasury Safekeeping Trust.

Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan.

Hegar graduated from A&M with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary's University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. ..

Gov. **Greg Abbott** will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." TOP NEWS "Texas A &M Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor," Bryan Eagle's Stephen Whitaker -- "The Texas A&M University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas ComptrollerGlenn Hegar as the 15th chancellor in system history. Hegar, a 1993 A&M graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. Trent Ashby , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M Foundation President Tyson Voelkel ; University of Alabama President Stuart Bell ; and U.S. Rep. Michael McCaul , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M grads. "The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M University System," Board of Regents Chairman Bill Mahomes said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. "Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System's impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish." Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1. "The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M University System is a tremendous honor," Hegar said in a statement. "I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence." The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current ChancellorJohn Sharp announced he would retire on June 30, 2025. Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon. "Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best." Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history. "I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M University System to heights that no one thought possible," Hegar said in a statement on X. A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston. "The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates," Mahomes said in a statement. "We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process." Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state's chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller's office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller's office also handles the state's cash and the state's Treasury Safekeeping Trust. Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan. Hegar graduated from A&M with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary's University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. .. Gov. Greg Abbott will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." * **TOP NEWS**

““**Texas A &M; Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor**,” Bryan Eagle's **Stephen Whitaker** — “The Texas A&M; University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas Comptroller**Glenn Hegar** as the 15th chancellor in system history.

Hegar, a 1993 A&M; graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. **Trent Ashby** , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M; Foundation President **Tyson Voelkel** ; University of Alabama President **Stuart Bell** ; and U.S. Rep. **Michael McCaul** , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M; grads.

“The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M; University System,” Board of Regents Chairman **Bill Mahomes** said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. “Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System’s impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish.”

Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1.

“The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M; University System is a tremendous honor,” Hegar said in a statement. “I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence.”

The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current Chancellor**John Sharp** announced he would retire on June 30, 2025.

Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon.

"Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M; system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best."

Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M; system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history.

“I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M; University System to heights that no one thought possible,” Hegar said in a statement on X.

A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston.

“The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates,” Mahomes said in a statement. “We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process.”

Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state’s chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller’s office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller’s office also handles the state’s cash and the state’s Treasury Safekeeping Trust.

Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan.

Hegar graduated from A&M; with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary’s University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. ..

Gov. **Greg Abbott** will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." With a decline in the number of credible news organizations, the Must Read Texas morning email is indispensable for anyone that wants to continue to be informed.” – Former U.S. Senator Phil Gramm (R-TX) | AUSTIN NEWSLETTER LAUNCHED Are you one of the 100,000+ subscribers to , a newsletter that delivers EVERYTHING you NEED TO KNOW about Austin? | Subscriptions are $5/mo, $50/yr or $199/lifetime here: . Read today's FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . | TOP NEWS ““Texas A &M; Board of Regents vote Glenn Hegar as sole finalist for chancellor,” Bryan Eagle's Stephen Whitaker — “The Texas A&M; University System Board of Regents voted unanimously on Friday to name Texas ComptrollerGlenn Hegar as the 15th chancellor in system history. Hegar, a 1993 A&M; graduate, was chosen over the other four finalists: Texas State Rep. Trent Ashby , R-Lufkin; Texas A&M; Foundation President Tyson Voelkel ; University of Alabama President Stuart Bell ; and U.S. Rep. Michael McCaul , R-Austin. All, except McCaul, were A&M; grads. “The Board is confident that Glenn Hegar is ready to usher in the next era of excellence at The Texas A&M; University System,” Board of Regents Chairman Bill Mahomes said in a statement. Mahomes declined further comment to media members at the meeting. “Hegar grasps the unique breadth and depth of the System’s impact on every corner of Texas through its eight state agencies and 11 universities. We, as members of the Board of Regents, are eager to see what he will accomplish.” » Texas state law requires a 21-day waiting period before the appointment can be made official. Once it becomes official, Hegar would begin as chancellor on July 1. “The opportunity to serve as the next chancellor of the Texas A&M; University System is a tremendous honor,” Hegar said in a statement. “I want to thank each of the regents for their confidence, yet more importantly for their energy, passion, dedication, and constant commitment to excellence.” » The process that led to Hegar being chosen as the next chancellor began last July when current ChancellorJohn Sharp announced he would retire on June 30, 2025. Sharp voiced his support for Hegar in a statement released Friday afternoon. "Glenn will make an outstanding chancellor for the Texas A&M; system," Sharp said. "He and Dara are devout Aggies and I am here to help in any way I can. I wish Glenn and his family the very best." Since taking over as chancellor in September 2011, Sharp has overseen several improvements to the system including the acquisition of a law school in Fort Worth and the creation of the RELLIS campus in Bryan. Sharp also worked to add the Texas Division of Emergency Management as the eighth state agency led by the A&M; system. Sharp is the longest-tenured chancellor in system history. “I want to thank Chancellor Sharp for his 14 years of strong leadership, as his steady hand has guided the Texas A&M; University System to heights that no one thought possible,” Hegar said in a statement on X. A national search was conducted by Russell Reynolds and Associates out of Houston. The five finalists were named in February and interviewed with the regents Feb. 24 at Russell Reynolds headquarters in downtown Houston. “The Board members were pleased to see its search for chancellor yield so much interest and so many qualified candidates,” Mahomes said in a statement. “We thank everyone who was involved in this extensive search and selection process.” Hegar has served as comptroller since 2014. The comptroller is the state’s chief tax collector, accountant, revenue estimator, treasurer and purchasing manager. The comptroller’s office is responsible for writing the checks and keeping the books for the state government. The comptroller’s office also handles the state’s cash and the state’s Treasury Safekeeping Trust. Before he was comptroller, Hegar served in the Texas Senate from 2006-14 and the Texas House from 2002-06. The Hockley native is a sixth-generation Texan. Hegar graduated from A&M; with a degree in political science and history. He has a master of arts and law degree from St. Mary’s University in San Antonio and a master of laws degree from the University of Arkansas. .. Gov. Greg Abbott will be tasked with choosing a replacement comptroller to finish out the current term before the next general election in November 2026." subject: Why this symbol ☮️ means peace
body:
In 1958, amid growing fears of Cold War nuclear proliferation, thousands of people gathered in London's Trafalgar Square on Good Friday.

| I n 1958, amid growing fears of Cold War nuclear proliferation, thousands of people gathered in London's Trafalgar Square on Good Friday. The demonstration featured the debut of an image that became one of the most recognized protest symbols in the world: the peace symbol.
